ops.title: capt-hook 9.10.0 blocks verbose comments (post-edit run size 3 lines/200 chars); cc-notes pack 0.11.0 pairs the ccn redirect; @latest pack sources read GitHub releases not main; nudges are skipped after a block — pair advisories via declarative hook()
ops.body:
Shipped 2026-07-13 (capt-hook 9.10.0 / general pack 0.21.0; cc-notes v0.30.0 / plugin 0.11.0), replacing the old max_fires=1 advisory comments nudge that everyone thought was "the hook that prevents verbose comments" — it warned once per session, measured only the introduced delta at 4 lines/300 chars, and no ccn redirect ever existed.

Current enforcement, proven live end-to-end via real dispatch in a scratch consumer repo:
- **Block** (`general.comments`): Edit/Write/MultiEdit leaving a non-doc comment run/block > 3 lines / 200 chars is DENIED. Post-edit total size for runs the edit created/modified (multiset-keyed; whitespace reflow exempt unless it crosses the threshold); untouched legacy runs exempt. Blank-line-separated comment paragraphs merge for the size check; trailing comments are singleton runs; shebangs excluded; doc runs require every line doc-marked (godoc adjacency covers grouped const/var, struct fields, imports, interface methods).
- **Warns**: doc-comment runs (exempt from block), comment-density (≥6 added lines, >50% non-doc comments; suppressed when the block already fired), and the cc-notes redirect (cc-notes pack) which rides INSIDE the deny under "Additional advisories (not the reason for the deny):".
- Accepted tradeoffs are listed in `captain_hook/packs/general/comments.py`'s docstring (go narration-above-decl = godoc; Write-based file moves re-trip on legacy comments; yaml/toml/md/json out of scope; exact boundaries permissive).

Two gotchas that will bite again:
1. **capt-hook `@latest` pack sources resolve to the latest GitHub RELEASE, not main** (packs/manager.py GITHUB_LATEST_RELEASE). Pushing a pack change with green CI delivers NOTHING to consumers until a release is cut — cc-notes needed v0.30.0 tagged just to serve the new hook.
2. **Since 9.10.0, dispatch skips handler-backed hooks (every `nudge()`, LLM or not) once a block has fired** — only DECLARATIVE `hook()` warns accumulate into a deny. Any advisory that must pair with a deny (like the ccn redirect) must be a declarative hook, not a nudge. Related: [[declarative-capt-hooks]], [[hook-multi-fire-was-multi-source-registration]].
